title: In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
subhead: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

title: War in Iran: At the G7, Canada and its partners want clarification from Washington
United States and Israel are facing the same Iranian foe but are fighting different wars
'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
subhead: The American president's disappointments and contradictory statements on the conflict in Iran obscure the key question of how to end the war, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: AI used to make 'fetishized' images of disabled women
subhead: British disability advocates have condemned an 'alarming trend' of AI-generated sexualized images of women and girls with disabilities and genetic conditions that are gaining millions of views on social media.

title: Napoleon's long-lost hat to go on display
subhead: Made by Poupard, Napoleon's official hatmaker, the headpiece displays all the hallmark features of an authentic imperial bicorne, including its distinctive proportions, a small tricolor cockade and a silk-taffeta lining. It will be exhibited at the Chateau de Chantilly, north of Paris, later in the year.

title: Cybercrime: The blind spot in museum security
subhead: Dozens of online ticketing systems in France's cultural sector were shut down following a large-scale cyberattack targeting Vivaticket, the leading operator in the field, on March 2. French institutions are still reviewing their defense systems.
byline: Damien Leloup,  Laurent Telo and  Roxana Azimi

Why the Greens lost several major cities in France's municipal elections
subhead: Although the Greens managed to retain Lyon, Grenoble and Tours, they failed to hold six municipalities which they won in 2020. A lack of ties to civil society and communication failures explain the losses of these narrow victories.

title: Zinédine Zidane closes in on leading Les Bleus
subhead: Less than three months before the 2026 World Cup, the former French maestro is the clear frontrunner to succeed France's football squad head coach Didier Deschamps, who will step down at the end of the competition.

title: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
subhead: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.
